title: Local RAG Playground
description: A fully local Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) platform that enables users to upload documents, perform semantic search, and chat with their data using local embeddings and LLMs. Built with FastAPI, Next.js, ChromaDB, and LM Studio, the system provides streaming responses, interactive PDF citations, and complete offline execution without relying on external AI APIs.
overview:
# Local Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Playground

A fully local, end-to-end Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline built with FastAPI, Next.js, and ChromaDB. This repository implements a robust ingestion, chunking, and retrieval architecture designed to eliminate external dependencies and provide complete control over the semantic search process.

## Architecture Overview

The system is separated into a Python/FastAPI backend that handles document processing, embeddings, and LLM inference via LM Studio, and a Next.js/React frontend that provides an interactive chat interface and PDF viewer.

```mermaid
graph TD
    %% Define Styles
    classDef client fill:#1e1e1e,stroke:#3b82f6,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff
    classDef api fill:#1e1e1e,stroke:#10b981,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff
    classDef processing fill:#2d2d2d,stroke:#f59e0b,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff
    classDef db fill:#1e1e1e,stroke:#8b5cf6,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff
    classDef external fill:#1e1e1e,stroke:#ec4899,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff

    subgraph "Client Layer (Next.js)"
        UI[Interactive UI]:::client
        Viewer[PDF Viewer]:::client
    end

    subgraph "API Layer (FastAPI)"
        IngestAPI[POST /api/v1/process]:::api
        AskAPI[POST /api/v1/ask/stream]:::api
    end

    subgraph "Ingestion Pipeline"
        Parser[PyMuPDF Parser]:::processing
        Graph[Graph Constructor]:::processing
        Chunker[Hierarchical Chunker]:::processing
        Embedder[SentenceTransformers BGE-Small]:::processing
    end

    subgraph "Storage"
        Chroma[(ChromaDB Vector Store)]:::db
        Registry[(Document Registry)]:::db
    end

    subgraph "Generation"
        LMStudio[LM Studio / LLM]:::external
    end

    UI -- Upload PDF --> IngestAPI
    UI -- Query --> AskAPI
    UI -. Fetch .-> Registry
    
    IngestAPI --> Parser
    Parser --> Graph
    Graph --> Chunker
    Chunker --> Embedder
    Embedder --> Chroma
    Parser --> Registry

    AskAPI --> Embedder
    Embedder --> Chroma
    Chroma -- Context Chunks --> AskAPI
    AskAPI --> LMStudio
    LMStudio -- SSE Streaming --> UI
    
    %% View Citations
    UI -. View Citation .-> Viewer
```

---

## Engineering Phases & Techniques

The repository was constructed across six distinct engineering phases, prioritizing local execution, semantic preservation, and memory robustness.

### Phase 1: Document Parsing & Canonical Model
* **Technique:** Standardization of input. All incoming documents are parsed into a uniform `Canonical Document Model` consisting of `Pages` and `Blocks`.
* **Details:** This abstraction allows the downstream pipeline to process PDFs, Word documents, or text files uniformly without writing format-specific chunking logic.

### Phase 2: Document Graph & Semantic Chunking
* **Technique:** Hierarchical Context Preservation.
* **Details:** Instead of naive character or token splitting (which destroys context), the pipeline builds a semantic graph of the document (e.g., Document -> Section -> Paragraph). Chunks are generated by flattening this graph, ensuring every chunk retains its full structural path (e.g., `Document > Introduction > Background`).

### Phase 3: Embedding Generation & Vector Storage
* **Technique:** Local Embeddings.
* **Details:** Utilizes `BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5` via the `sentence-transformers` library running entirely in-memory. Vectors and metadata are persisted locally using `ChromaDB`.

### Phase 4: Retrieval & LLM Generation
* **Technique:** Server-Sent Events (SSE) Streaming.
* **Details:** Connects to any local LLM hosted via LM Studio (using OpenAI-compatible endpoints). The FastAPI server yields streaming tokens back to the frontend in real-time, preventing timeout bottlenecks on complex generations.

### Phase 5: Interactive UI & Document Management
* **Technique:** Citation Highlighting and State Syncing.
* **Details:** The React frontend parses citation metadata returned by the LLM. Clicking a citation dynamically renders the original PDF and jumps to the exact source page.

### Phase 6: Robust Parsing & Ephemeral Sessions
* **Technique:** C-based Parsing & Auto-wiping.
* **Details:** Replaced heavy ML-based OCR pipelines (like Docling) with `PyMuPDF` to eliminate `std::bad_alloc` out-of-memory errors on complex slide decks. Implemented session-level database wiping on server restart to prevent stale context conflicts.

---

## Directory Structure

```text
d:\Repos\RAG
├── app/                       # FastAPI Backend
│   ├── api/                   # REST Endpoints (Routes & Dependencies)
│   ├── chunking/              # Hierarchical Chunk Builder
│   ├── graph/                 # Semantic Graph Constructor
│   ├── models/                # Pydantic Data Models (Document, Block, Chunk)
│   ├── parsers/               # Document Parsers (PyMuPDF)
│   ├── services/              # Business Logic (Ingestion, Registry, LLM)
│   └── vectorstore/           # ChromaDB Integration
│
├── frontend/                  # Next.js Frontend
│   ├── public/                
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── app/               # Next.js App Router (page.tsx, globals.css)
│   │   └── components/        # React Components (UploadButton.tsx)
│
├── documents/                 # Ephemeral Document Storage
│   ├── files/                 # Raw PDFs
│   └── metadata/              # JSON Registry files
│
├── chroma_db/                 # ChromaDB SQLite Storage
├── config.yaml                # Global Configuration (LLM, Embedding, Chunk Size)
└── requirements.txt           # Python Dependencies
```

---

## Setup & Run Instructions

### Prerequisites
1. **Python 3.10+**
2. **Node.js 18+**
3. **LM Studio** (Running a local LLM, e.g., `qwen2.5-7b-instruct`)

### 1. Configure LM Studio
1. Open LM Studio and start the Local Inference Server.
2. Ensure the server is running on `http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1`.
3. Verify your model ID in `config.yaml` matches the loaded model.

### 2. Start the Backend (FastAPI)
Open a terminal in the root directory:

```bash
# Create and activate a virtual environment
python -m venv venv
.\venv\Scripts\activate  # Windows

# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install PyMuPDF

# Run the FastAPI server
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
```
*Note: The first startup may take a few moments as it downloads the `bge-small` embedding weights.*

### 3. Start the Frontend (Next.js)
Open a new terminal in the `frontend` directory:

```bash
cd frontend

# Install Node dependencies
npm install

# Start the development server
npm run dev
```

### 4. Usage
1. Navigate to `http://localhost:3000` in your browser.
2. Wait for the "Waking up the Backend" overlay to disappear (indicating the embedding models are loaded in memory).
3. Upload a PDF using the "Upload Document" button.
4. Chat with your document. Toggle the "RAG Assist" switch to compare responses with and without retrieved context.
